$|K(j\omega)|=\frac{1}{\sqrt{ 1+(\omega RC)^{2} }}$

$K_{L}(\omega)=20\log |K(j\omega)|$

$K_{L}(\omega)=0-20\log \sqrt{ 1+(\omega RC)^{2} }$
$\omega\ll \frac{1}{RC} \implies K_{L}(\omega)=0$
$\omega\gg \frac{1}{RC}\implies K_{L}(\omega)=-20\log(\omega RC)$

stroke-dasharray="8 9"></path></g></g><mask></mask><g transform="translate(15.014416805896985 239.1875452195767) rotate(0 27.299972534179688 12.5)"><text x="0" y="17.619999999999997" font-family="Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji" font-size="20px" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="start" style="white-space: pre;" direction="ltr" dominant-baseline="alphabetic">20dB</text></g></svg>
  $|K(j\omega)|=10·\frac{\sqrt{ 1+\omega^{2} }}{1+(\omega 10)^{2}}$
  $K_{l}(\omega)=\underbrace{ 20\log 10 }_{ 20dB }+\underbrace{ 20\log \sqrt{1+\omega^{2}} }_{ K_{L}^{(1)} }-\underbrace{ 20\log \sqrt{ 1+(\omega 10)^{2} } }_{ K_{L}^{(2)} }$
$\omega\ll_{1}\implies K_{L}^{(1)}=0$
$\omega\gg_{1}\implies K_{L}^{(1)}=20\log(\omega)$

$\omega\ll \frac{1}{10}\implies K_{L}^{(2)}=0$
$\omega\gg \frac{1}{10}\implies K_{L}^{(2)}=20\log(\omega)$
